dtcs.description: 62, U0124/95, U0126/63: Control Module Communication Bus OFF

Lost Communication with Steering Angle Sensor Module If 2 or more DTCs are output during the DTC check, one side of the CAN branch wire may be open (One side of the CANH [branch wire]/CANL [branch wire] of the ECU and/or sensor is open). NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL (a) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal before measuring the resistances of the main wire and the branch wire.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (STEERING ANGLE SENSOR BRANCH WIRE) (a) Disconnect the E11 steering angle sensor connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE STEERING ANGLE SENSOR BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CANH, CANL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEERING ANGLE SENSOR - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization (b) Disconnect the E11 steering angle sensor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE STEERING ANGLE SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Steering Angle Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal Vehicle Stability Control System

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Clearance Sonar Module HINT For vehicles with the intuitive parking assist system only. NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting Meter / Gauge System

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (CLEARANCE WARNING ECU BRANCH WIRE) (a) Disconnect the F12 clearance warning ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CLEARANCE WARNING ECU BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CANH, CANL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CLEARANCE WARNING ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization (b) Disconnect the F12 clearance warning ECU connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE CLEARANCE WARNING ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Navigation System

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with ECM / PCM "A" The combination meter communicates with the ECM via the CAN line. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check for DTCs and note any codes that are output    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Recheck for DTCs    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If CAN communication system DTC U0100 and another DTC are output at the same time, perform troubleshooting for CAN communication system DTC U0100 first. B --  Go to CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting Vehicle Stability Control System

dtcs.description: 38 - Lost Communication with 4WD Control ECU NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL (a) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal before measuring the resistances of the main wire and the branch wire.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (4WD CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE) (a) Disconnect the A27 4WD control ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE 4WD CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CAN+, CAN-) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (4WD CONTROL ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization (b) Disconnect the A28 4WD control ECU connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE 4WD CONTROL ECU  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Relays and Modules - Transmission and Drivetrain/Relays and Modules - Transfer Case/Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Vehicle Stability Control System

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Vehicle Stability Control Module

NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL (a) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal before measuring the resistances of the main wire and the branch wire.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (SKID CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE) (a) Disconnect the A24 master cylinder solenoid (skid control ECU) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CANH, CANL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization (b) Disconnect the A24 and A25 master cylinder solenoid (skid control ECU) connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE MASTER CYLINDER SOLENOID (SKID CONTROL ECU)  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly Pre-Collision System

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Airbag ECU The seat belt control ECU receives signals from the master cylinder solenoid (skid control ECU) and the center airbag sensor via the CAN communication line. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Use the Techstream to check if the CAN communication system is functioning normally    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting. OK: DTC is not output. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (DIAGNOSIS SYSTEM)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting CAN Communication System

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Instrument Panel Cluster Control Module (Combination Meter) When the clearance warning ECU self-diagnosis determines that the CAN communication system is malfunctioning, this DTC is stored. HINT When 2 or more DTCs starting with "U" are output simultaneously, inspect the connectors and wire harness of each ECU. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Intuitive Parking Assist System. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Intuitive Parking Assist System. Result B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure A --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions Navigation System

HINT *1: w/ Pre-Collision System *2: w/ Intuitive Parking Assist System

CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Seat Belt Control ECU HINT For vehicles with the pre-collision system only. NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL (a) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal before measuring the resistances of the main wire and the branch wire.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE) (a) Disconnect the E42 seat belt control ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CANH, CANL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization (b) Disconnect the E41 and E42 seat belt control ECU connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal CAN Communication System
